Cut numeric variables into intervals

Source: R/cutR.R
cutR.Rd

Wrapper for cut() with simplified labels and automatic conversion to factor based on most frequent levels

Usage

cutR(
  data,
  vars,
  seq.list,
  name.list = list(),
  name.pattern = NULL,
  simplify = T,
  digits = 0,
  dt = F
)

Arguments

data

dataframe. Works with piping.

vars

Vector of vars which should be cut

seq.list

List of the split patterns for each var. E.g. list("v1" = seq(0,10), "v2" = round(quantile(df %>% pull(age), seq(0,1,0.1)),0)).\ Can be specified using only "median", "quantile", "quartile" etc which will be applied to all vars

name.list

list of names for new variables in the format: list("new" = "old")

name.pattern

naming pattern that should automatically be pasted on the end of all the specified variable names in "vars".

simplify

whether date intervals should be simplified

digits

number of digits for label formatting

dt

whether a data.table should be returned (default = F)

Value

The inputted dataframe with the cut variables

Examples

redcap_df %>%
  datR(c(birth, date_of_surgery)) %>%
  cutR(vars = c(age, birth, size, type, date_of_surgery),
       seq.list = list(age = "10y",
                       birth = "year",
                       size = "quartile",
                       type = c(0,2,10),
                       date_of_surgery = "quarter"))
#>      id sex   age birth   followup date_of_surgery  size type localisation
#> 1     1   1 70-80  1929 03-04-2023        2008, q4  2-14  0-2            3
#> 2     2   2 30-40  1953 18-12-2025        1991, q4 14-24  0-2            1
#> 3     3   1 60-70  1948 21-09-2025        2008, q4 38-50  0-2            2
#> 4     4   1 40-50  1949 11-02-2022        1994, q3 24-38  0-2            3
#> 5     5   2 40-50  1966 21-04-2022        2005, q3 24-38 2-10            1
#> 6     6   1 80-90  1925 17-07-2023        2007, q1 38-50 2-10            3
#> 7     7   1 70-80  1921 15-11-2023        1996, q2 38-50  0-2            2
#> 8     8   1 30-40  1960 07-10-2021        1999, q3  2-14  0-2            5
#> 9     9   2 30-40  1958 11-03-2025        1990, q3  2-14  0-2            3
#> 10   10   2 50-60  1953 28-11-2021        2004, q4 14-24 2-10            1
#> 11   11   1 60-70  1946 09-06-2023        2008, q1  2-14  0-2            3
#> 12   12   1 70-80  1927 24-04-2024        1999, q2 38-50 2-10            2
#> 13   13   1 10-20  1980 01-06-2022        1992, q2 24-38 2-10            3
